instructions.name: accept_task
docs: Provider accepts the task, moving escrow to Active status.

instructions.name: approve_milestone
docs: Client approves a submitted milestone, releasing funds.
`rating` (0..=5) is the client's per-task rating of the provider's
work. It is folded into `avg_rating` in the registry via the
reputation CPI when the final milestone of the escrow is approved.
Pass 0 when no rating is given; non-zero values below or above the
0..=5 band are rejected with `InvalidRating`. See finding #8 in
ARCHITECTURE_DEEP_CRITIQUE.md for the pre-fix dead-state behavior.

accounts.name: registry_program
docs: Agent Registry program for CPI reputation updates.

accounts.name: provider_profile
docs: Must match seeds [escrow.provider, "agent-profile"] under the Registry program.

accounts.name: settlement_authority
docs: Settlement authority PDA — this program's signing authority for CPI calls.
The Registry program verifies this PDA as a signer with seeds::program = SETTLEMENT_PROGRAM_ID.

accounts.name: protocol_config
docs: Finding #19: Reads the governance-owned `reputation_delta_task_completed`.

instructions.name: cancel_escrow
docs: Client can cancel an escrow that hasn't been accepted yet.

instructions.name: close_escrow
docs: Close a terminal-state escrow and reclaim rent to the client.

instructions.name: create_escrow
docs: Creates a new task escrow with defined milestones.

accounts.name: client_vault
docs: Finding #21: Client's canonical vault PDA, owned by the Agent Vault
program. Seeds `[b"vault", client]` match `InitializeVault` in the
vault program. Anchor enforces the seed constraint, so an attacker
cannot substitute an unrelated Pubkey. The account need not exist
(no `init`, no data read) — we only bind the stored `client_vault`
address to the canonical cross-program derivation.

accounts.name: provider_vault
docs: Finding #21: Provider's canonical vault PDA, owned by the Agent Vault
program. Same rationale as `client_vault` — off-chain consumers (MCP,
discovery, indexer) can trust the stored `provider_vault` address
because the runtime enforced its derivation at escrow creation.

accounts.name: provider
docs: The provider's public key

accounts.name: token_mint
docs: Token mint (e.g., USDC)

accounts.name: client_token_account
docs: Client's token account to transfer from

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Escrow state account — dynamically sized based on milestone count.
Space formula: 298 + (milestones_data.len() * 41)
Fixed: 8 (disc) + 160 (5 pubkeys) + 16 (amounts) + 4 (vec prefix)
+ 1 (status) + 8 (task_id) + 32 (desc_hash) + 16 (timestamps)
+ 33 (dispute_resolver Option) + 9 (disputed_at Option) + 1 (bump)
+ 10 (margin) = 298
Per milestone: 32 (desc_hash) + 8 (amount) + 1 (status) = 41

accounts.name: escrow_token_account
docs: Escrow token account (ATA of escrow PDA)

accounts.name: protocol_config
docs: Finding #19: Singleton protocol config. Read to enforce
`min_escrow_amount` without a program upgrade. Must be initialized
via `initialize_protocol_config` before any escrow can be created.

instructions.name: expire_escrow
docs: Anyone can expire an escrow that has passed its deadline.

accounts.name: provider_token_account
docs: Provider's token account for releasing earned milestone funds on expiry.

accounts.name: registry_program
docs: ADR-050: Registry accounts for slashing on expiry with undelivered milestones

accounts.name: protocol_config
docs: Finding #19: Reads the governance-owned
`reputation_delta_expiry_undelivered` when an expiry slashes.

instructions.name: initialize_protocol_config
docs: Finding #19: One-shot initialization of the `ProtocolConfig` PDA.
Must be called once per program deployment before any escrow can be
created. The `payer` becomes the initial governance authority.

instructions.name: raise_dispute
docs: Either client or provider raises a dispute.

instructions.name: reject_milestone
docs: Client rejects a milestone, setting it back to Pending for re-work.

instructions.name: resolve_dispute
docs: The dispute_resolver (or client) resolves a dispute.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Finding #20: Authorization hoisted from handler to account-level
constraint for visibility parity with Registry's `has_one = authority`
pattern. Anchor's `has_one` cannot express OR-logic against an
`Option<Pubkey>`, so a `constraint` clause is used. The dispute.rs
handler still derives `is_resolver` for the slash decision (A-03),
but the authorization gate itself is enforced here.

accounts.name: registry_program
docs: ADR-039: Registry program for slashing reputation on dispute

accounts.name: provider_profile
docs: Must match seeds [escrow.provider, "agent-profile"] under the Registry program.

accounts.name: protocol_config
docs: Finding #19: Reads the governance-owned `reputation_delta_dispute_loss`.

instructions.name: resolve_dispute_timeout
docs: ADR-030: Auto-resolve a dispute after timeout.

accounts.name: registry_program
docs: ADR-050: Registry accounts for slashing on timeout

accounts.name: protocol_config
docs: Finding #19: Reads the governance-owned `dispute_timeout_seconds`
and `reputation_delta_dispute_loss` used here.

instructions.name: submit_milestone
docs: Provider marks a milestone as submitted (proof of work).

instructions.name: update_protocol_config
docs: Finding #19: Authority-gated update of the governance-owned tunables.
Any `Option::None` field is left unchanged. See
`instructions::update_protocol_config` for sanity-bound details.

errors.name: InvalidMilestoneCount
errors.msg: Invalid milestone count: must be between 1 and 5

errors.name: MilestoneAmountMismatch
errors.msg: Milestone amounts do not sum to total escrow amount

errors.name: DeadlineInPast
errors.msg: Deadline cannot be in the past

errors.name: InvalidStatus
errors.msg: Invalid escrow status for this operation

errors.name: UnauthorizedProvider
errors.msg: Only the provider can perform this action

errors.name: UnauthorizedClient
errors.msg: Only the client can perform this action

errors.name: InvalidMilestoneIndex
errors.msg: Invalid milestone index

errors.name: InvalidMilestoneStatus
errors.msg: Invalid milestone status for this operation

errors.name: AmountOverflow
errors.msg: Amount overflow detected

errors.name: UnauthorizedDispute
errors.msg: Only authorized party can raise a dispute

errors.name: AlreadyDisputed
errors.msg: Escrow is already in dispute

errors.name: EscrowExpired
errors.msg: Escrow has expired

errors.name: UnauthorizedResolver
errors.msg: Only dispute resolver or client can resolve disputes

errors.name: InvalidRefundAmount
errors.msg: Refund amounts do not match remaining escrow balance

errors.name: InvalidTokenAccount
errors.msg: Token account does not match expected mint or owner

errors.name: DeadlinePassed
errors.msg: Task deadline has passed

errors.name: DeadlineNotReached
errors.msg: Deadline has not been reached yet

errors.name: InvalidAmount
errors.msg: Invalid amount: must be greater than zero

errors.name: InvalidRegistryProgram
errors.msg: Invalid registry program

errors.name: BelowMinimumEscrow
errors.msg: Escrow amount below minimum (anti-sybil)

errors.name: SelfDealingProhibited
errors.msg: Client and provider cannot be the same account

errors.name: DisputeTimeoutNotReached
errors.msg: Dispute timeout has not been reached yet

errors.name: InvalidDisputeResolver
errors.msg: Dispute resolver must be a neutral third party — not the client or provider

errors.name: InvalidRating
errors.msg: Rating must be in 0..=5 (0 = no rating)

errors.name: UnauthorizedConfigAuthority
errors.msg: Unauthorized: must be ProtocolConfig authority

errors.name: InvalidProtocolConfigValue
errors.msg: Invalid ProtocolConfig value: violates sanity bounds

types.name: ProtocolConfig
docs: Finding #19 (ARCHITECTURE_DEEP_CRITIQUE): On-chain governance parameters
for the Settlement program. Before this account existed, the economic
tunables (minimum escrow, dispute timeout, reputation deltas) were
compile-time constants — changing any of them required a full program
redeploy. Now they live in a single PDA owned by this program and can
be updated by `update_protocol_config` from an authority-gated tx.
This is an *interim* governance path. A richer DAO/multisig scheme is
out of scope; the `authority` field can be rotated to any key — a
timelock or multisig program's PDA would be a natural upgrade target.

fields.name: authority
docs: Key authorized to run `update_protocol_config`. Can be rotated.

fields.name: min_escrow_amount
docs: ADR-028: minimum escrow amount in token base units. Anti-sybil floor.

fields.name: dispute_timeout_seconds
docs: ADR-030: seconds between `raise_dispute` and the earliest timestamp
at which `resolve_dispute_timeout` may auto-resolve.

fields.name: reputation_delta_task_completed
docs: ADR-039: positive delta applied via CPI when the final milestone is approved.

fields.name: reputation_delta_dispute_loss
docs: ADR-039: negative delta applied via CPI when a dispute resolves against the provider.

fields.name: reputation_delta_expiry_undelivered
docs: ADR-050: negative delta applied via CPI when an escrow expires with undelivered milestones.

fields.name: bump
docs: PDA bump for re-derivation.

types.name: ProtocolConfigInitialized
docs: Finding #19: emitted when `ProtocolConfig` is first created. The initial
values are a snapshot of the compile-time defaults.

types.name: ProtocolConfigUpdated
docs: Finding #19: emitted when `update_protocol_config` successfully mutates
the on-chain tunables. Indexers should key off this to drive dashboards.

fields.name: disputed_at
docs: ADR-047: Timestamp when dispute was raised. None if not disputed.
Uses Option<i64> instead of sentinel 0 for proper null semantics.
